task_completion.description: Access the JFrog Artifactory login page
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the JFrog Artifactory login screen at http://localhost:8082.

task_completion.description: Log into Artifactory as the admin user
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifactory dashboard or welcome screen is visible, indicating a successful login (often showing 'admin' in the user profile section).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Repositories management section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Administration module is open, specifically showing the 'Repositories' -> 'Local' (or similar) settings page.
The repository named 'helix-staging-local' is visible in the list or table of repositories.

task_completion.description: Initiate the deletion of the target repository
task_completion.visual_evidence: A confirmation dialog, modal, or action menu is visible, explicitly asking to confirm the deletion of 'helix-staging-local'.

task_completion.description: Verify the target repository has been deleted
task_completion.visual_evidence: The repository list is shown again, and 'helix-staging-local' is no longer present, while 'example-repo-local' is still visible.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a web browser to interact with the Artifactory GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a standard web browser (like Chrome or Firefox) navigating the Artifactory web interface, rather than terminal windows executing curl commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the interaction is with the genuine JFrog Artifactory application
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI elements, styling, and layout should match JFrog Artifactory, not a generic text editor or mocked HTML page.

integrity.description: Verify the default repository was not mistakenly deleted
integrity.visual_evidence: The repository 'example-repo-local' should remain visible in the repository list in the final screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target repository to delete is 'helix-staging-local'. It contains the artifact 'org/apache/commons/commons-lang3/3.14.0/commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar', which will be destroyed when the repository is deleted. The environment also contains a default repository named 'example-repo-local' which must remain intact.
